question.title: Cumulative Sales by Product
question.desc: Write a SQL query to calculate the cumulative sales for each product over the last 3 days. Use the 'sales' table to find the daily sales and the 'products' table to get the product names.
question.setup: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS sales; CREATE TABLE sales (product_id INT, sales_date DATE, amount DECIMAL(10, 2)); INSERT INTO sales (product_id, sales_date, amount) VALUES (1, '2022-01-01', 100.00), (1, '2022-01-02', 120.00), (1, '2022-01-03', 110.00), (2, '2022-01-01', 50.00), (2, '2022-01-02', 60.00), (2, '2022-01-03', 70.00); DROP TABLE IF EXISTS products; CREATE TABLE products (id INT, name VARCHAR(255)); INSERT INTO products (id, name) VALUES (1, 'Product A'), (2, 'Product B');
question.solution: WITH daily_sales AS (SELECT p.name AS product_name, s.sales_date, SUM(s.amount) AS daily_amount FROM sales s JOIN products p ON s.product_id = p.id GROUP BY p.name, s.sales_date) SELECT product_name, sales_date, SUM(daily_amount) OVER (PARTITION BY product_name ORDER BY sales_date) AS cumulative_sales FROM daily_sales ORDER BY product_name, sales_date;
question.tips: Using a Common Table Expression (CTE) can simplify complex queries.
hints: Start by joining the 'sales' and 'products' tables on the 'product_id' column.
Use a window function to calculate the cumulative sales for each product.
You'll need to partition the results by 'product_name' and order by 'sales_date' to get the correct cumulative sales.

tests.setup: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS sales; CREATE TABLE sales (product_id INT, sales_date DATE, amount DECIMAL(10, 2)); INSERT INTO sales (product_id, sales_date, amount) VALUES (1, '2022-02-01', 200.00), (1, '2022-02-02', 220.00), (1, '2022-02-03', 230.00), (2, '2022-02-01', 80.00), (2, '2022-02-02', 90.00), (2, '2022-02-03', 100.00); DROP TABLE IF EXISTS products; CREATE TABLE products (id INT, name VARCHAR(255)); INSERT INTO products (id, name) VALUES (1, 'Product C'), (2, 'Product D');

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS sales; CREATE TABLE sales (product_id INT, sales_date DATE, amount DECIMAL(10, 2)); INSERT INTO sales (product_id, sales_date, amount) VALUES (1, '2022-03-01', 300.00), (1, '2022-03-02', 320.00), (1, '2022-03-03', 330.00), (2, '2022-03-01', 120.00), (2, '2022-03-02', 130.00), (2, '2022-03-03', 140.00); DROP TABLE IF EXISTS products; CREATE TABLE products (id INT, name VARCHAR(255)); INSERT INTO products (id, name) VALUES (1, 'Product E'), (2, 'Product F');
